required_edits: Repair the shared lexer/classifier so regex-versus-division is decided from actual previous significant token context across all frozen expression-start contexts, including default-export and class-extends expressions, without treating keyword-named properties or methods as control/keyword contexts. Add classifier and closure regressions proving regex-contained load lookalikes create no edge and do not cause closure failure, while genuine adjacent loads remain visible.

Enforce b3 for every standard line-leading whitespace form admitted by the profile, not only space and tab, and add stable-diagnostic tests including form-feed or another non-space/tab ECMAScript whitespace character.

Complete b6 truncated-form detection, including a bare `import`, and add tests requiring `ProfileError` with diagnostic exactly `unsupported-module-lexical-profile`; audit the other accepted-form prefixes so none can return a successful no-edge classification when truncated.
hard_stops: AM-41 supported-profile correctness is not satisfied. The regex-versus-division logic is context-insensitive for required expression-start contexts. For example, `export default /import("\x2e\x2fghost.mjs")/;` is valid supported-profile source whose regex contains only a load lookalike, but `default` is absent from `REGEX_KEYWORDS`; the lexer treats the opening slash as division and `classifyModuleLoads` consequently infers a literal dynamic-import edge to `./ghost.mjs`. Closure derivation then falsely includes or fatally resolves that target. Similar defects exist around `extends` and keyword-named member calls because control-head and keyword decisions use only `prevSigWord`, without establishing token context.

AM-41's exact fail-closed set is incompletely enforced. A line-leading legacy close comment preceded by standard whitespace other than space/tab, such as form-feed (`\f-->`), is b3 and must throw `unsupported-module-lexical-profile`; the implementation checks only U+0020 and tab and accepts it. Truncated-form detection is also not total: a bare `import` is accepted as producing no sites rather than rejected as a truncated accepted module-load form under b6.
rationale: The implementation otherwise tracks much of frozen Task 4a faithfully: the AM-40 package roots and exclusions, normative inventory-id table, deeply frozen D32 mapping, git/code-only implementation inventories, closure equality, counted iterators, containment helpers, exact git commands, and four code-edge endpoint shapes are present and tested. The deterministic gate passing does not cure the uncovered AM-41 counterexamples. Because D33 closure provenance depends directly on this classifier, a false regex edge can make a committed closure wrong or turn valid supported-profile source into a fatal unresolved target; this is a substantive faithfulness failure, not merely missing test coverage.
